reviews: Any review saying she isn't a good professor or can't teach is wrong.She cares about your learning and offers many opportunities to boost your grade.Her lectures were pretty good, and I'd say we learned a lot more compared to Jasmine's classes. However, the ALEKS homework was very time-consuming, and her exams were pretty difficult. Slow grader too

Any review saying she isn't a good professor or can't teach is wrong. She cares about your learning and offers many opportunities to boost your grade. Her lectures were pretty good, and I'd say we learned a lot more compared to Jasmine's classes. However, the ALEKS homework was very time-consuming, and her exams were pretty difficult.

Dr.Pesce was really stressful. She is judgemental of questions in the moment and will make you feel unintelligent, and then a second later say "I had a great question from them". She's a touch grader on her quizzes too. If she likes you, she will be nice but really tough in general. She's slightly better than Jazmin. Only take if you have to.

She is honestly a really good teacher, if you put in the work and pay attention to her lectures, you WILL leave her class actually knowing what your doing (which is great if you need to take ochem 2). Only issue is that she yaps a LOT and will get distracted or go off on tangents, which can be annoying. And her grading can lag behind a little.
She's just so. mean. Do not take her. Extremely unaccessible outside of class, I once waited for 40 minutes for her to see me during office hours. She's so rude. Just don't.

Grades take a long time to be returned and emails often go unanswered. The class can feel frustrating due to lack of clarity and support. Not the most approachable teaching style, so expect to figure out a lot on your own. I would recommend choosing a different instructor if possible.
Loved her class so much. Completely engaging and interesting
so condescending i would not recommend her to anyone!! extremely unhelpful and is weirdly rude to students who ask questions that she doesn't like
Not a great professor! Honestly surprised there are good reviews. Condescending to students when asking questions and will single u out constantly if you make small mistakes.

Dr Pesce is one of the greatest orgo professors here. She seemed a bit intimidating initially, but she was nice during office hours and gave me useful tips for studying for exams. Her class does require a lot of work to do well though.
If there is a professor with a better rating available, please do yourself a favor and TAKE THE OTHER PROFESSOR.
the way she makes chemistry interesting is amazzinggg

Amazing and interactive lectures. Would definitely take her class again if got a chance. Shoutout to her for making me turn from anti-chemistry person to a chemistry lover. I took this gened chemistry of wine and loved the class

She's super fun to listen to. Her lectures are always interactive and she often connects the coursework with movies and sit-coms. I took her chemistry of wine class and it was amazing. We also had frequent demo sessions during classtime. We had the option to do the final presentation rather than giving the final exam. We got quizzes every week.

TERRIBLE. if you are sensitive to sound, do not take her as she yells and generally talks very loudly. otherwise, condescending. often brags about how poorly her students do on her exams. maybe she's good for other classes but she seems to enjoy making fun of her students in orgo.
shes literally just mean to kids who ask questions, doesn't really even answer questions asked and does not teach.

so bad. do NOT TAKE HER. so loud and barely teaches. forces students to stay for an entire 3 hour lab even if ur done. lab reports are acc harder than actual chem labs. stares students down and call kids out and be extremely passive aggressive for literally no reason at all. insufferable and cant stop talking about orgo tests are usually easy ish

Her reviews are not accurate in my opinion. She responded to all of the emails I sent her the same day, is AMAZING in office hours, lectures are super engaging and lots of good examples. Exams are just like the practice exams. Can be passive aggressive if you ask a question she thinks is dumb, so watch out for that.
I MEANT FINAL EXAM CAN ONLY BENEFIT YOU, if you don't pass it, she'll just pretend it never happened.

Easy to keep up with.She's clear, loud, and seems to love teaching the chem behind wine. There's a quiz every week but there's two chances. Final grade can only benefit you, if not, never happened. Near end, gives redemption week, make up work. Recitation- for group project, just present research on wine article.Rude to disruptive students.

If you have a choice between her and another professor, pick the other professor. She is not accessible by email, and is frequently traveling. In lecture, she's decent at explaining concepts but horrendous when it comes to questions. Often makes an example out of students and gets annoyed when students ask questions.

I'm shocked that she has such negative ratings. I'm a first semester freshman and I plan on taking her again next semester. I haven't talked to her one on one but I'd feel comfortable enough if it's ever needed. She answers questions in class and is great with discussing the answers, the only issue I've seen her have is with disruptive students.
horrible stay away

She. is. so. loud. and. obnoxious. New professor but not young. failed out of Penn!!! be warned. talks a lot about how "she's the best at what she does". just saying, if you keep having to convince yourself and others of that, maybe its not true? decent lecturer but poor communicator. takes a long time to grade assignments, doesn't answer questions

Would avoid if possible. She is fine enough during lectures but creates a toxic environment. She is incapable of answering questions, && she embarrasses whoever asks the question. She rarely answers her emails, and gets upset when people come to her office hours. Doesn't post due dates & takes long time to grade. So arrogant & condescending

Lectures are fine enough. Biggest problem comes from her attitude towards students. Condescending and arrogant. Hates being asked questions and often doesn't answer the questions. Does not respond to emails. Very often ends up talking about her latest running adventures in office hours or her boyfriend. Gets annoyed when students don't understand

Her lectures are pretty good, but she can definitely come off condescending when asked questions. She really is not as bad as these reviews make her out to be. Offers extra credit and the grading is straight forward.

very mean about questions, makes an example out of you in front of the class if you ask a question in order to embarrass you. Takes so long to grade assignments, and respond to emails. Its a hard class already, but even harder when you can't ask questions. If you have to take her class, at least try to find one where she's not the recitation too.

Terrible at communication, sent her a very important email last week and never responded. Sent her a second email to follow up and responded with a thumbs-up emoji. Assignments are tedious and overall very unhelpful.

She is one of the worst professors I have ever had the misfortune of having.  Rude to students who ask questions. Doesn't post due dates, late grading exams. When asked for updates about exam grades, she is very rude. Does offer extra credit, but isn't available outside of class hours for help. Beware, bad reviews on this page keep getting removed.

The good reviews of her are so dishonest, they could only be coming from Professor Pesce herself. Extremely difficult course and she is not available outside of class hours (and honestly during class hours) for help.

AVOID AT ALL COSTS! All these poor reviews are honest and TRUE! She is not, caring AT ALL, this unkind soul actually thinks she can teach SOOOO GOOOD that the students shouldn't have any questions. Don't take her if you want to do well, and have a professor to help WHEN PAID TO DO SO!!!!!!!!!!!!!

Tends to be very rude to students and does not create a good environment for learning. She stopped posting due dates at the end of the year. She slams tons of information on you the lecture before the exam and then makes that half of the exam.
Any professor who laughs in the faces of students when they asks questions should not be a professor.

Please do not listen to any of the negative feedback. This is my second time taking orgo 1 and she's probably the best at Temple. She gives you every opportunity to be successful. Yes, she is a little intimidating, but don't let that shy you away. Regardless, of my grade in class, i've really learned a lot. would 100% take her again.
This professor does not respond to email.

Honestly, way too hard of a class for a gen-ed fulfilling course (and I'm a chem major).  She was arrogant and used a lot of big phrases like "I know what I'm talking about" which is bold to say of a self-declared "new prof". Loud, talked too much, and didn't really take the time to answer questions. Would avoid her if possible. Unprofessional

I would say if you have an option to take someone else, you should. She's new to teaching, which isn't inherently a bad thing but it is for her.  I did totally fine in this class but I found that she was condescending anytime someone asked a question. Talks very, very loudly to the point its distracting and headache inducing. Not the best

Would never recommend this professor. She was beyond condescending. Spent more time talking loudly just to hear herself talk than teaching. Extremely arrogant and unhelpful when answering questions, Has office hours but is rude and belitting when asked questions. Self describes herself as "a fantastic teacher" but didn't answer our questions

She is a very good professor and really knows how to reach well. She's also very understanding of any accommodations a student may need and she definitely wants everyone to succeed. She sometimes appears to be intimidating, but she doesn't mean to be. Don't let that stop you from going to office hours.

She's a very smart woman and definitely a good professor considering she has extensive teaching experience doing MCAT and DAT prep with the Princeton Review. Chem 1032 is still a really tough topic so most people struggled, but she was very helpful if you had a specific question. She is kind of intimidating and can come off as condescending, though
